key_insight: PDEFlow automates the full PDE-to-neural-operator workflow by converting natural-language problem descriptions into validated specifications, solver-generated datasets, and checkpoint-based solver-free inference.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, repeatable surrogate modeling for solid mechanics and other physics problems where many related PDE configurations must be simulated and queried with minimal manual effort.
method: Stateful input graph turns multi-turn user edits into a validated PDE/ODE specification.
FEniCSx-based finite-element backend samples parameters and generates operator-ready solution tensors.
Registry-based training/inference interface supports interchangeable neural operators; demonstrated with multi-branch Bayesian DeepONet.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports benchmark ODE and PDE experiments showing valid specification construction, solver-backed dataset generation, training across steady and transient problems, and successful checkpoint-based inference.
watch_item: Performance and usability depend on the fidelity of the FEniCSx solver setup and on whether the registry-based operator interface generalizes beyond the demonstrated Bayesian DeepONet implementation.
paper_title: PDEFlow: Autonomous Agentic PDE Pipelines for Neural Operator Learning and Solver-Free Inference
